response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a highly experienced Staff Software Engineer with over 15 years of diverse industry experience, making him severely overqualified for an entry-level Junior Java Developer role. While he has touched Java in past roles and demonstrates strong agile and TDD practices, his primary stack is TypeScript/Node.js/DLang — not Java. More critically, the role requires strong German communication skills, and the candidate self-rates German as only 'Basic,' which is a hard blocker for a client-facing consulting position in Germany. There is also no insurance sector experience whatsoever.
strengths: Solid CS degree and broad polyglot engineering background with demonstrated Java exposure at Altom Consulting
Strong agile and software craftsmanship practices (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development) well evidenced across multiple roles
Proven ability to collaborate with clients and lead technical initiatives across complex projects
concerns: Severely overqualified — 15+ years of staff/senior-level experience for an entry-level position signals retention and motivation risk
German proficiency is self-rated 'Basic,' failing the hard requirement for strong verbal and written German in a client-facing consulting role
Java is a peripheral skill; primary expertise is TypeScript/Node.js/DLang with no recent Java-centric project work
